name: Poly Multisample
description: This algorithm is inspired by the original “SD Multisample” algorithm on the disting EX, with hints of the Crossfade Loop Synth 103 VST plug-in, and the “Audio Playback with Crossfade” algorithm on the disting mk4. It is a polyphonic sample playback instrument, playing WAV files from the MicroSD card. It can also record audio (i.e. sample in the traditional sense) and play that. It can apply crossfade looping to make smooth looping pads out of any sample material. It can be played via CV/gate, MIDI, or I2C. It supports both velocity switches and round robins per sample.
gui_description: The display shows the notes being played by the various voices, each superimposed on a bar to indicate the note envelope. The folder and filename of the last sample played are also shown. When adjusting the microtuning parameters, more information about the chosen tuning is displayed. When adjusting trim and loop points, this algorithm uses the UI described for the sample players.

specifications.name: Record time
specifications.description: The maximum length of audio that can be recorded (sampled).

parameters.name: Folder
parameters.description: Sets the sample folder to use.

parameters.name: Sample
parameters.description: Allows you to choose a single sample within the folder to play, or “<MULTISAMPLE>” for the usual case where you want the algorithm to choose the sample automatically based on the note pitch.

parameters.name: Gain
parameters.description: Sets the output level.

parameters.name: Pan
parameters.description: Sets the stereo pan position.

parameters.name: Transpose
parameters.description: Adjusts the tuning in semitones.

parameters.name: Fine tune
parameters.description: Adjusts the tuning in cents.

parameters.name: Crossfade Mode
parameters.description: Sets the crossfade looping mode: Off, Forwards, or Alternating.

parameters.name: Crossfade Length
parameters.description: Sets the length of the crossfade, as a percentage of the loop time (the difference between the loop point and end trim point).

parameters.name: Loop point coarse
parameters.description: Sets the loop point, as a percentage of the sample length.

parameters.name: Loop point fine
parameters.description: Adjusts the loop point, in sample frames.

parameters.name: Sustain mode
parameters.description: The standard polysynth sustain mode parameter.

parameters.name: Sustain
parameters.description: Directly controls the sustain (like a MIDI sustain pedal).

parameters.name: Gate offset
parameters.description: Offsets (delays) the gate inputs relative to the pitch inputs. This is useful to allow pitch CVs to settle before they are sampled on the rising gate, and also to cope with modules which output both a pitch and gate but change their gate first.

parameters.name: Round robin mode
parameters.description: The round-robin mode.

parameters.name: Loop
parameters.description: Sets whether the sample will loop. ‘From WAV file’ will loop if the sample has loop points defined in the file and not otherwise. ‘Off’ and ‘On’ force the sample to loop or not.

parameters.name: Bend range
parameters.description: The MIDI pitch bend range.

parameters.name: Confirm change
parameters.description: Sets whether the folder and/or sample changes immediately or requires confirmation.

parameters.name: Envelope
parameters.description: Enables an ADSR volume envelope. If the envelope is disabled, the sample simply plays once until the end.

parameters.name: Attack
parameters.description: Sets the envelope attack time (from 1ms to 15s, exponential response).

parameters.name: Decay
parameters.description: Sets the envelope decay time (from 20ms to 15s, exponential response).

parameters.name: Sustain Level
parameters.description: Sets the envelope sustain level.

parameters.name: Release
parameters.description: Sets the envelope release time (from 10ms to 30s, exponential response).

parameters.name: Velocity
parameters.description: Sets the amount by which the note velocity affects the note volume.

parameters.name: Press -> volume
parameters.description: The amount by which pressure (per note for MPE) affects the note volume.

parameters.name: MPE Y -> volume
parameters.description: The amount by which the MPE Y dimension affects the note volume.

parameters.name: MIDI channel
parameters.description: The MIDI channel to listen on.

parameters.name: MPE channels
parameters.description: Controls how the algorithm will respond to MPE.

parameters.name: I2C channel
parameters.description: Sets the I2C channel.

input_ports.name: CV/gate
input_ports.description: Control voltage and gate inputs for pitch and triggering.

input_ports.name: MIDI
input_ports.description: MIDI input for note and control data.

output_ports.name: Left/mono output
output_ports.description: The left or mono output audio signal.

output_ports.name: Left/mono output
output_ports.description: The left or mono output bus.
